VerbalDescription: Find the shape that is the topmost shape. Set the grid dimensions to the same height and width of the shape. Copy only that shape into the output. Use the exact shape, and color of the shape. Do not completely fill the grid if that shape would normally not do so. (Anything but a square or rectangle.
Resize the grid to match the top shape. Fill in the top shape in the new resized grid
There are 2 shapes in the input. Which ever shape is on top, replicate that and make the whole grid size the extent of that shape
Resize the grid to the height and width of the top shape on your example. Then copy the top shape into that grid, dont copy the bottom shape.
Reproduce the shape that it on the top of the original grid. Make the new grid the same size as this shape i.e. so that the shape identified fills the whole grid. Keep the shape the same colour.
Identify the object that is furthest to the top of the grid in input and set grid size to same size. Recreate the object in the same colour, leaving all other squares black.
Change the grid size to 2x3. Based on the task demonstration, there is a pattern. Yellow, green, green, yellow. Given this pattern, fill in the grid with yellow.
Take the top shape and create grid the size the shape will just fit in to. Then copy that shape into the grid keeping it the same colour.

The input will have two shapes, one on top and one on bottom. Resize the output to have the dimensions of the shape on top. Then, draw the shape on top in the output (same shape and color, the shape on top will be your output).
Create a rectangle containing only the top figure in its entirety, keeping color.
Resize the grid to the same size as the shape on top. Then change the colour to match the shape. Include any black squares if needed
Resize the grid to fit exactly the top-most item from the input, and then replicate the exact pattern of that top-most item

Look for the top pattern on the example and replicate this exactly while discarding any patterns below the top pattern. Then match the cell dimensions to the size of the pattern. if it is not a flat or sqaure pattern you will need to make the cell demensions fit the pointed point so you can end up with some blank cells if this is the case otherwise all the cells should be filled as it is the size of the pattern itself.

There will be 2 distinct shapes on your screen, each of a different colour. The solution is whichever of your shapes is the top one. The solution is solely the size of your shape, so when creating your grid make sure it is only the size of your shape.
Take the top shape, Make the grid to just fit the shape into and copy the top shape into the grid keeping the colour the same.

There should be a yellow "shape" and a green "shape" in the input. The output should be a copy of only the upper one of these shapes. Make the output grid large enough to draw that whole shape, but no larger.

Two objects are visible on black background. Take only the top object, Width x Length, and make the grid size the same WxL. Copy exactly only the top object from input. Use same colors and layout. There will be no black border or anything surrounding this object.
The uppermost object is selected. It is the only object. It moves up one square and to the left on square. The grid is resized until it disappears behind the object.
Make the grid to the size of the top most shape and fill the grid with that shape in the same color as the original.
Identify the upper object in the image.  Then make a grid that is the same size as this object and copy the object exactly into this grid.
Adjust the grid size to fit only the top shape of the example, in the tightest way you can. Recreate that top shape with the same color, so that it's contained in the new smaller box
The top shape becomes the solution so for this one make the grid 3x3 and fill it so it's all green then put a black square in the middle.
Resize your grid to 3x3, flood fill all squares green, select the center square and it black, submit.
Keep the grid the same, swap the top and bottom shapes with each other and keep the middle shapes the same
Keep the grid size the same. Flip the top colored block with the bottom colored block, but keep the two middle colored blocks the same.

You will need to copy the original grid size.  In the grid there will be four shapes arranged vertically.  All four shapes will stay the same size and color.  You are changing the location of the top and bottom shapes.  The bottom shape must change location with the top shape.  You will place the upper left corner in the same position as the top shape.  You will keep the bottom shape the same color and shape.  Next you will take the second shape and place it directly below the new shape.  The upper left corner will be in the square from its horizontal position.   You will place the next middle shape below.  You will then place the shape that had been on the top below the second middle shape.  Its upper left corner will start inthe same horizontal position.  Each shape will be the same size and color as the original shapes.

Use the same size grid as the original, and keep the position of the overall object within the grid the same. Move the top coloured shape to the bottom, then add the 3rd from top on top of that, followed by the 2nd from top, followed by 4th from top.

The rule is that the shaopes at the top and the bottom of the grid should be swapped. So you will need to rezie the grid andd copy the input grid by clocking on the copy input grid button. You should then swap the top and bottom shapes by using the edit function. In this case the shapes are identical, and all that needs to be chagned is the colour, so you should use the edit function to change the colours around - the top colour should be swapped with the bottom colour.

Maintain the same grid dimensions from the input. In the stack of shapes, take the first and last shapes and swap them, putting the bottom shape on the top and the top shape on the bottom, keeping the shapes the same size and color. Allign new top of the top shape(bottom shape from input) where the top of the new bottom shape was. Next, repeat this process swapping the opposite shapes until there are none left to swap.
You need to flip the original image upside down, and if there are black squares at the top in the original the stay at the top when you flip the image,
There will be a shape of multiple colours. Keep the grid the same size as the input, keep the colours the same but flip the shape upside down.
Copy grid size and copy the shape and colours but turn it upside down

keep grid size the same as the input. Take the shape that is most towards the top and change it's place with the shape that is most towards the bottom. Move any pieces in the middle up or down enough to accommodate the changed places but no more.
I follow the task demonstration
Swap the top most and bottom most color and shape.
change the height and width as in the lower object  as the input and copy the object as in input.
pick a sensible symbol with colors
Resize the grid to fit only the lower most shape, make it fit in as small a grid as possible, keep colors the same.
Resize the grid to the size of the lowest down shape in example one. Recreate this shape in the new grid with the same color it previously appeared.
Take the shape at the bottom and adjust the grid so it is the same width and height as the shape. Remove all other cells outside of the width and height of the bottom shape
Shrink the grid to the smallest size that will fit the bottom shape, and fill it with the bottom shape in the same color.
Create the smallest possible grid around the lower shape only on the original then fill in the coloring.
Take the lower shape only and replicate that on its own no other cells.
Change grid to fit bottom shape. Do not change size or color and cut out everything else.
Identify the shape closest to the bottom of the grid. Then resize your grid to the exact dimensions of this and colour accordingly.
Use a grid with a height of 3 and width of 1. Place the pale blue object there filling the whole 3x1 grid.
Recreate the lower shape with the minimum of black squares around it to still communicate the shape. keep the size and colour the same.

There are two shapes in this, copy the bottom shape only and copy the colors. Make the grid the size of the bottom shape. If the shape is irregular, it's okay, just make sure that the grid's size will fit the shape and leave the empty blocks to be black.

There will be two objects in the input. Copy the shape that is below the first shape and use the same colour as the shape at the bottom. Make sure your grid size is exactly the same as the width and height of where the shape at the bottom is coloured in.

Recreate the bottom shape exactly the same size and colour as in the original image.  Make a grid size exactly equal to the shape so that it fills it to it's maximum to the longest point horizontally or vertically.

Use the same size grid as the input.. In your answers, at every intersection where blocks overlap, the vertical color block must overlap the horizontal color block. Keep all colors the same except the overlapping block which must have the same color as the vertical blocks
Copy out the size of the grid from the shape nearest the bottom of the input. Then copy that shape onto the new grid.
copy input, make the top row of grey squares red
keep everything the same, but replace the top line of the object with the reddish orange color

Firstly, resize the output grid to the same dimensions of the input grid. Then, draw the shape presented to you in exactly the same location, using exactly the same dimensions. Your transformation is to change the top line of the shape completely to the colour red, ensuring that the other parts of the shape remain in the original colour.
Copy the input grid. Select the top row of coloured blocks and change their colour to red.
Use the same size grid as original, recreate the original shape's size and position but all of the top row change to bright red colour

Replicate the same pattern as the example using the same cell dimensions and the same cell colour as the example, however the top line should be changed to a light red regardless of what colour the example is.
copy the puzzle to your solution. replace the top line of colored shape in red color
I follow the task demonstration
The grid and the object stay the same. A salmon colored row in added to the top of the object in place of the object's initial color.
Make the top line of the shape red
Make the top line of the shape RED

Use the same grid size as the input, There will be one object in the input which you should replicate fully as it is in terms of position and colour. Once this is complete, colour the top row of the shape in red.
Copy the dimensions of the grid. Copy the shape exactly, but make the top row of your shape red instead of the colour identified on the original picture, the rest of it can be the original colour.

Resize the grid to the same size as the task demonstration grid. Select the edit button and the correct colour as the demonstration. The rule is that the colour on the demonstration, the top line colour of the demonstration diagram must be changed to red.
Copy the input grid and then color only the top row of the input object red.
Copy the test input.  The top horizontal row of colored squared of the shape should be replaced with red squares.
The top row of the pattern was colored red.

Resize the grid to match the width and height of the input box. There is one item in the input box. Copy the item into the same position in the target box. Change the color of the topmost horizontal line to red. Use the same color as the put box for the rest of the item shape.
Start by copying from input. Select the dark orange colour and colour in all of the top line of squares of the shape in this colour.

Keep grid size the same as input. Object should remain in the same position and in the same colour except for the top row of squares of the object, change colour to red for the top row of squares for the object.
Keep grid size and object colour/position the same as input. Change the top row of squares of the object to red.
Copy the grid over and change the top row of the colored shape to red. Leave the rest of the colors the same.
Edit the dimensions to be the same as shown in the test. Take the top row of coloured boxes and change it to red. Then complete the shape as normal
Use the same grid size from the example. Remove any shapes that are touching the outer walls of the grid. Keep the shapes that aren't touching the outer walls the same shape and color as the example.
I follow the task demonstration
You will see a number of shapes in a vertical orientation. Copy the grid from the example then remove the topmost and bottom-most shapes. The middle shapes remain unchanged.
copy the input grid size. keep the center most shapes and clear the one topmost and one bottommost shapes.
copy the input grid size. delete the topmost and bottommost block, leaving just the center block/blocks

Make the grid the same height and width as the original. Copy the objects in the same location and color as the original, but do not copy the top object or the bottom object. Only the object or objects in the center need to be copied over.
Use the same grid size as in the input. There will be multiple objects. Simply remove the Top Object and the bottom object only.
Find the middle grids then fill with colors
The top and bottom shape are both removed from the image and turned into black cells. The grid and positioning of shapes remains indenticaly.
Duplicate the image. Delete the top and bottom shapes in the column that are different colors. Keep all other shapes the same.

You need to copy the original grid size.  In the grid there will be a group of shapes lined up vertically.   You need to remove the top and bottom shape.  Any shape or shapes in the middle will have the same position, size, and color.  You need to copy those shapes.
Copy across, then remove the top and bottom shapes.
Copy the grid as the input, but remove the colours that are on the top of the grid and on the bottom of the grid.

Copy the gird size and shapes exactly as in the input. Erase one shape at the top and one shape at the bottom by filling the cells with black and keep the shapes in the middle in their original color.
Keep the same grid size and image. All you need to do is remove the shape that is the highest on the grid and remove the shape that is on the lowest on the grid. That's all!

Resize the grid to the same size as input, copy the shapes size and colour from the input but don't include the shape at the top and bottom and only include the middle shapes (if there is only two shapes, put no shapes in).

Keep the grid size the same as shown. You will have several shapes on your screen and what you need to do is only copy the shapes which are positioned in between the two symmetrically identical shapes; if there are no shapes in between the two symmetrical shapes, then you will leave the grid blank. The colours and size of the shapes should remain the same.
Use the same grid size as the input. There will be one object in the input, in your answer, eliminate the first and last objects( keep its colour the same).
Keep the same grid size. Eliminate all shapes except the one that is the absolute lowest on the grid.
Copy from the input grid, and then erase all of the objects except for the bottom-most object by flood-filling those objects with the color black.
Use the same size grid.  Find the lowest shape on the grid and copy this across to the new grid, using the same colour.  Do not copy any other shapes across, just the lowest.
Leave the image nly at the bottom of the input
Press the copy from input button. Remove all the shapes by changing the colour to black, apart from the shape which is closest to the bottom of the grid.
With the same size grid, leave only the lowest object in the same place and remove every other object.

Change the grid size to the input size. There will be different objects in the grid. Locate the object that is the lowest in the grid and remove all other objects. Keep the color of the lowest object the same.
Use the same grid size.  There will be multiple objects in the input.  Only copy the object that is closest to the bottom of the grid.  Keep the color the same.
The grid needs to be 10x10. Only replicate over the bottom most shape, keeping its original colour. Ignore any higher shapes.
Copy grid size and shapes but delete all shapes apart from the one which is the closest to the bottom of the grid
Remove all other shapes besides the shape closest to the bottom of the grid
Keep the same size grid, but only put the bottommost shape on the grid in the same location from the right.
Resize grid to match one on the left. Use select to copy precise color(s) of lowest object shown on grid.
copy the rid and image over exactly then change to black all the object except for the one that is closest to the bottom of the frame
Use a 9x9 grid. Transpose only the bottom most shape exactly into the new grid
keep grid the same size, just keep the lowest shape in the same postion as it is, remove all other shapes
copy the grid and then remove everything except from the the lowest shape and colour combination on the grid
Same size grid. Determine which colour shape is the lowest on the grid. All other shapes should be removed so only the lowest shape remains. Colour of this shape does not change
copy from the input grid, and recreat only the lowest color grid
use the same grid as in the input. There will be multiple objects in the input; in your answer, use the same grid as in the input, but draw only the object which is below all else in the input
arranged it according to size while paying attention to the gap in between.
Of all the shapes pick the one that is the furthest down. Recreate that shape and remove the rest and fill black. All the rest stay the same.
Copy over the bottom or leftmost shape only, making the grid size equal to the shape copied.
nice
You want to flood fill a grid that corresponds to the same shape and size and color as the shape that is closest to the bottom.
Make the grid the size of the space taken up by the smallest object in the input and recreate that object in your new grid.
Identify the shape that is lowest on your grid (i.e., closest to the bottom), resize your grid so it the exact dimensions of that shape and colour it accordingly.
Focus on the pink object. Use the same grid size as the pink object. In your answer, the colour remains unchanged (pink), as does the size. Flood the grid in pink.
set grid to 2x2 and fill in with purple

There will be two objects in the input. Identify the object whose lowest cell has a lower vertical position than the lowest cell of the other object, then resize the grid to the exact same size as the object you identified. Finally, draw only the object you identified in the grid.
Find out the grids which is below then input the exact grid color.

There will be two shapes in the input, one positioned lower than the other. Note the lower shape, and make your grid only as big as needed to contain a copy of it. Duplicate the lower shape in your answer.
the is a shape lower than the other. Make the grid the size of that lower shape. No place that shape on that grid in the same color.

There should be 2 shapes. Pick the shape that is lower down and adjust the height and width based on the height and width of the bottom shape. Replicate the shape and colour exactly and keep background black
Look for the shape closest to the bottom of the grid. Resize the grid so it matches the height and width of the bottom shape in the test input grid. Redraw that shape in the exact same colour.
grid 5 x 6, make every edge square red so as to match the red shape
Resize the grid to match the width and height of the bottom-most object, then draw that object into the output area using its color.
Find the lower shape, resize the grid to the same size as this, then copy the shape across and keep the same shape and colour.
Take the shape that is positioned lowest on the grid and replicate it (including colour) and make the final grid only big enough to hold that shape. (E.g if shape is a 2x3 “L” then make the grid 2x3)

Ok so in this case the rule seems to be that the object closest to the bottom of the grid takes up the entire square. In this case that was the red square (with the empty centre). 

To solve this then what I did was reshape the grid to the dimensions of the shape (5X6) and then recreate the shape in that grid. I did this by pressing the edit button and colouring the blocks in red (as that was the colour of the shape)
grid resized to lowest coloured block, then lowest coloured block put in grid.
Resize the grid to re-create only the top shape.
Take whatever shape is higher up and resize the grid to that shape, then fill it in with the color of that shape
There will be two objects in the input. Note the upper shape, and make your answer grid the same size and color.
Only keep the top line, and make the grid only the size of the top line. Use the same color as the top line.
condense the grid to show ONLY the top object as it appears in the input
Find the top shape and just make the grid the same size, shape and color as the top shape only.
Resize the grid to the size of the topmost object and flood fill with same color.
There will be 2 horizontal stripes of 2 different colors.  Copy only the top horizontal stripe for the output.  Keep the color the same as the top stripe.
Note which object is higher, in the input grid. Create output grid to match the size of that object and color to match.
Take the topmost object from the input grid, and resize the grid so that it has the same dimensions as the object. Change all blocks of the grid to the colour of the object.
Create a canvas of the size of the upper figure, copy the figure
Reproduce only the shape at the top of the original grid i.e. the highest up shape. Make the grid the exact size of this shape. Keep the same colour and size of the shape.
Resize the grid to fit the object that is closest to the top exactly. Copy the objects as is to the new grid.
Find the line or shape that is higher in the grid.  Resize the grid to match that line or shape and then color the whole grid in the same color.
Create the grid from the shape that is on top and paint it the same color as that shape.
Create a grid which is the same height and width as the highest shape on the original grid. Colour this grid in to match the highest shape.
Make the grid match the size of the object that is highest on the grid. The object stays the same size, shape and colour. The other object is not included.
the higher shape in the input grid is what the grid will become. make the grid the same size as the highest shape and use the same colour for all the spaces to make a copy of that shape

Resize the grid to the same size as the test grid. Find the color of the object lowest on the grid, this will be the color of all the objects. Fill in the objects in the same location as the test grid using the color of the lowest shape on the test grid.
Keep shape sin same position. The color of the shape at the bottom becomes the color of the other shapes.

Resize the grid to 10x10. There are multiple items in the input box. One item is touching the outside edge of the grid. Using only the color of that item, reproduce all the items from the input box in the same size and location.
Use the same input. Find the object that is the lowest one (on the lowest row). Use the color of that object, and make all the objects match  that one's color.
Expand the grid to match the sample.  Copy the shapes exactly as in the sample.  Color all of them with the color of the shape that touches the bottom of the sample.

Use the same size grid as the original, and retain all the original coloured shapes in their same position. Look at the colour of the bottom-most object. Change the colour of all other objects to that same colour.
Resize the grid to the same size as the original. Keep all of the same shapes/patterns. Colour them all the same, in that of the lowest shape/pattern.

You will select 'copy from input'. You are now going to change every box that is red or yellow into a teal blue box by clicking on the teal color at the bottom and then choosing 'edit'. You will then drag your cursor along as you select every box to make them all teal blue.
Use the same grid size as the input. Replicate the exact same object size and position from the input but change the colour of each shape to the colour of the shape at the bottom of the input.
Copy the image exactly as it is. The only difference is that the colour of the shapes all need to be made the same as the colour that is closest to the bottom edge.

Resize the grid so it is a 10x10 square, the same as the target. The target will have multiple identical shapes. Recreate those shapes in the target grid. However, in the test, identify the color of the lowest shape, and make EVERY shape in the target grid that color so the final image is monochromatic
Set grid size to 10 by 10. Identify the color at the bottom of the grid. Transfer all objects into the 10 by 10 grid and make them the same color as the identified color at the bottom of the grid.
Keep grid size and object positions the same. Identify the colour of the object furthest at the bottom of the grid and colour all objects in this colour.

Adjust the dimensions of the shape to be the same as the test. Whatever the colour is of the bottom shape. Adjust the colour of all the other shapes to be the same as that colour. Complete in the same positions
Copy the full grid over but make all the shapes the same colour as the bottom shape.
NICE

Copy the exact grid size and copy every shape over to the new grid. Locate the color of the shape in the original that is at the bottom of the grid. Use that color for every one of the shapes in the new grid. Disregard all other colors.
Make all shapes the same color as the bottom shape.
Use the same grid size. Find the lowest object or object touching bottom of grid.  Duplicate all objects using bottom objects color.
copy the grid and arrangement from the example but then alter the pattern so that all coloured squares are the same colour as the lowest shape on the grid
Make your grid the same size.  Copy the shapes and colour them all the same colour as the bottom shape on the demo.
Exactly copy the image changing only the color of the first stripe to grey.
Copy the grid exactly as it is. There should be colored (not black) lines of some sort. Pick the top most colored line, and change all of the colored boxes into gray boxes.
Copy the input grid. If the row of the rightmost tile of the topmost object has a colored tile, then color the rightmost object gray. Otherwise color the leftmost object at the top gray.

Make the grid the same size and make the design the exact same with the colors and pattern. Then fill the top most section of the actual pattern (not the black color filled part) and use the gray on that section.

Resize the grid to the test input grid size. Select the edit button and the appropriate colours. The rule is that the top line of the shape, whether this be horizontal or diagonal or in a block shape in the corner and change this colour input to silver.
Copy the input grid. Change the colour of the top line of the pattern to grey.
Make the top line of the pattern grey, leave the same at the usual colours.
Copy over the grid size and the object placement. Change the color of the top colored line to gray. If there is more than one possible line pick the one that surrounds a black tile.

Set the grid to 10x10. The objects in the grid are going to mirror images of whats available to us. So in our case we fill in three rows of orange in a diagonal way. The rule here to fill in the furthest row of colored pixels that are away from the rest of the others as grey. So you will be left with two rows of orange pixels below the grey row of pixels.
Either the first or the smallest shape near the top not black becomes gray.
use the same size grid as the input. copy the shapes and colours in the input, except grey out the "top" shape in the grid instead of having it in it's normal colour
Adjust the dimensions to be the same as the test. Copy the patterns as shown in the test but change the first pattern to grey. Copy the rest of the pattern in the original colour
Resize the grid to the same size as input, copy the shapes that are shown, make the top line/shape grey coloured but for all others use the same colour as shown.
Keep the same grid. Recreate the demonstration. Take the shape closest to the top  and color it in grey.
Copy the input grid to the output grid; recolor the cells of the uppermost pattern (the one most "north" in the input grid to the color gray.
Copy grid from input. Color the top most colored (nonblack or grey) object in the input grey.

Start by changing the dimensions of your grid to match the input test grid size. Copy the 3 shapes from the test input grid. The colours of the bottom 2 shapes remains the same. The colour of the top shape needs to change to grey.

Using the same size grid as the input, recreate any shapes/patterns seen in the input exactly - but for any shape/patterns that touch the top most row in the grid change the colour to grey. All other shapes/patterns retain their existing colour. If more than one shape/pattern touches the top of the grid, change the colour of the smallest one
Make the grid the same height and width as the original. Copy the design using the same colors except the top green L shape needs to be colored gray.
